bio.fun_fact: Two-time winner of the NHL's Lady Byng Memorial Trophy for sportsmanship and gentlemanly conduct combined with a high standard of playing ability.
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Position: Defenseman
  • Son of Robert and Wendi Slavin
  • Two-time winner of Lady Byng Memorial Trophy
  • His sister, Jordan, played hockey at the University of North Dakota
  • His brother, Josiah, also played hockey at Colorado College
Biographical Information

Jaccob Slavin grew up one of five siblings in Erie, Colorado, a suburb of Denver. Slavin and his siblings would all play hockey and took advantage of the Colorado weather, once building an igloo in their backyard during spring break.

Slavin played college hockey for two seasons at Colorado College after being drafted by the Carolina Hurricanes in the fourth round of the 2012 NHL Draft. Slavin has spent his entire professional career with the Hurricanes and twice been named the Lady Byng Memorial Trophy winner. Slavin holds franchise records for postseason games played, goals, assists and points among defensemen.

Slavin first represented the U.S. at the U20 IIHF World Championships in 2014, and next was called up to the senior team for the 4 Nations Face-Off in 2025. 

Career Highlights
  • World Junior Championship, Malmö, 2014 (Team - Men)
Olympic Experience
  • 1-time Olympian; 1-time Olympic medalist (1 gold)
    • Olympic Games Milan 2026, gold (Team - Men)

bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Went to college at the University of Minnesota, played club volleyball while there
  • Married to Julie (Motz) Slick. The couple has a son named Edison (Eddie) born in 2016 and a daughter named Delaney born in 2018
  • Was selected to the Minnesota All-State Choir in his junior and senior years of high school
Career Highlights
  • Has two career international gold medals, both on the NORCECA Continental Tour
  • Partnered with two Olympic gold medalists during his career, Dain Blanton and Todd Rogers
  • ---
  • DID YOU KNOW?
  • Played in three events that were part of the 2019 FIVB World Tour with Casey Patterson. Placed ninth in Qinzhou and Yangzhou, China
  • Played in nine 2018 FIVB World Tour events with Casey Patterson. Best finish was third at Tokyo three-star
  • Played in six 2018 AVP events with Casey Patterson. Placed third in Austin
  • Suffered a partially detached retina in his left eye in 2017 on the AVP Tour, which is why he wears protective goggles while playing
  • In 2017, had a best of 17th place on the FIVB World Tour, bronze on the NORCECA Continental Tour; on the domestic AVP Tour, won one tournament and had five Top 3 finishes his best season on the Tour
  • Made his FIVB World Tour debut in 2013; since then, has a career best finish of fourth in 2015
  • Made his international debut in 2010 on the NORCECA Continental Tour; Between 2010-16 had two gold, four silver and two bronze
  • On the AVP Tour from 2009-16 has five bronze medals
  • In 2010, made his first AVP main draw with Olympian Dain Blanton in Blanton's final pro beach event
  • Slick made his pro beach debut in 2009 at AVP Chicago with partner Ryan Vander Veen
  • Member of the 2009 USA Volleyball Beach High Performance Team (U26)
More
View Stafford Slick's full career results at bvbinfo
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2019 – 9th (Team - Men)
  • Years of participation: Team - Men 2019
  • Top finish: 9th – 2019 (Team - Men)

bio.education: St. Johns College High School Towson University
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Grew up with five siblings, played a few different sports in high school and ended up playing Division I soccer in college
  • After graduating, searched for new sports to compete in – participated in Crossfit, Strongman competitions and triathlons
  • Ended up giving handball a try after recommendations from friends who play in other countries
  • Her full- time job is running her own health & sports performance business, Elite Wellness Performance and Recovery, which has given her the flexibility to train and surround herself with the latest fitness technologies to help with her athletic performance
  • Loves to compete and having something to train for, so playing for Team USA is a dream come true and something she has been preparing for her whole life.
Career Highlights
  • 2021: Named to U.S. Women's National Team for North American and Caribbean Handball Confederation (NACHC) Women's Championships

  • Has three sons, Samuel, Brooks and Sawyer and one daughter, Amelia

  • Has been a professional water polo player since 2005
  • Hobbies include swimming, surfing, reading, writing, business and hanging with friends and family

bio.education: Our Lady of Mercy Academy (Syosset, N.Y.) ‘11 Sacred Heart University ’15, Psychology; Sacred Heart University ’16, Master of Education
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Smith played all sorts of sports growing up in order to keep up with her very athletically talented siblings. She was inspired to row when she attended high school at Our Lady of Mercy Academy and they offered rowing as a varsity sport. She took up rowing because she believed it was a sport that she would be able to excel in. She says that living out her dreams of rowing on the United States National Team would not be possible without the continuous love and support of her family and friends, but specifically her parents, grandparents, and Coach, Nicoleta Mantescu
  • Daughter of James and Annmarie Smith
  • Has one older brother, James, a younger brother, Brian, and a younger sister, Caleigh
  • Favors all New York sports teams; the Yankees, Giants, Rangers, and Knicks
  • Favorite book is "Hope Solo's Memoir" and her favorite TV show is "Friday Night Lights"
  • Assistant coach for the Sacred Heart University Rowing Team.
Paralympic Experience
  • 1-time Paralympian; 1-time Paralympic medalist (1 silver)
    • Paralympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, silver (Legs, Trunk & Arms Coxed Four - Mixed)
World Championship Experience
  • Most recent: 2019 – gold (PR3 Pair - Women)
  • Years of participation: PR3 Pair - Women 2018, 2019; PR3 Coxed Four - Mixed 2017; Legs, Trunk & Arms Coxed Four - Mixed 2013, 2014, 2015
  • Medals: 5 (2 gold, 3 silver)
    • Gold – 2019 (PR3 Pair - Women); 2018 (PR3 Pair - Women)
    • Silver – 2017 (PR3 Coxed Four - Mixed); 2015 (Legs, Trunk & Arms Coxed Four - Mixed); 2014 (Legs, Trunk & Arms Coxed Four - Mixed)

bio.biography:
Quick Facts

  • Specialty: Backstroke, Butterfly, Freestyle

Biographical Information

Regan Smith is a U.S. swimmer, specializing in backstroke, butterfly, and freestyle. A world record holder and Olympic medalist, Smith has established herself as a key figure on the U.S. national swimming team.

Smith was born to a family who encouraged her swimming career. Smith began swimming at the age of two when her parents signed her up for swimming lessons to learn water safety. Smith quickly showed promise in backstroke events and won her first race at the age of seven. 

She achieved the world record in the 100-meter backstroke (long course) and junior world record in the 200m backstroke (long course) and has demonstrated versatility across multiple strokes.

Outside of swimming, she is known for her academic focus and has expressed interest in marine biology. She enjoys spending time with friends, participating in water sports, bowling, and watching ice skating and gymnastics.

Olympic Highlights 

Smith's Olympic debut at the Tokyo 2020 Olympic Games showcased her ability to perform under pressure, earning medals in both individual and relay events. There she earned a silver medal in the 200m butterfly, a bronze medal in the 100m backstroke, and a silver medal as part of Team USA's 4x100m medley relay team.

At the Paris 2024 Olympic Games, Regan Smith captured three silver medals in an impressive showing, reaching the podium in the 200m butterfly final, 100m backstroke final, and 200m backstroke final.

World Championships 

At the 2019 world championships in Gwangju, Regan Smith made history with an extraordinary performance, claiming gold in both the 200m backstroke final and 4x100m medley relay final, while setting world records in both the 100m backstroke (during the semifinals) and 200m backstroke.

Three years later at the 2022 world championships in Budapest, Smith continued her dominance in the backstroke events, securing gold medals in both the 100-meter backstroke final and as part of the 4x100m medley relay team.

Early Career

She attended Lakeville North High School, where she set multiple state records. Smith's breakthrough on the international stage came at the 2017 world junior championships, where she won multiple gold medals and set junior world records.

Her collegiate career at Stanford University was marked by NCAA titles and record-breaking performances. Smith has successfully balanced the demands of collegiate swimming with her international career, showcasing her versatility and dedication to the sport.

Smith trained at Stanford University, refining her technique and race strategy. Smith's smooth, efficient stroke and powerful underwater work contribute significantly to her success.

Smith continued to be a vital member of the U.S. national swimming team while competing for Stanford University. Later, Smith transferred to Arizona State University, where she continued to win medals and set personal bests.

Olympic Experience
  • 2-time Olympian; 8-time Olympic medalist (2 gold, 5 silver, 1 bronze)
    • Olympic Games Paris 2024, gold (4 x 100m Medley Relay - Mixed, 4 x 100m Medley Relay - Women), silver (100m Backstroke - Women, 200m Backstroke - Women, 200m Butterfly - Women)
    • Olympic Games Tokyo 2020, silver (200m Butterfly - Women, 4 x 100m Medley Relay - Women), bronze (100m Backstroke - Women), 5th (4 x 100m Medley Relay - Mixed)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2023 – gold (4 x 100m Medley Relay - Women), silver (100m Backstroke - Women, 200m Backstroke - Women, 50m Backstroke - Women), bronze (200m Butterfly - Women)
  • Years of participation: 100m Backstroke - Women 2023; 200m Backstroke - Women 2017, 2023; 200m Butterfly - Women 2023; 4 x 100m Medley Relay - Women 2023; 50m Backstroke - Women 2023
  • Medals: 5 (1 gold, 3 silver, 1 bronze)
    • Gold – 2023 (4 x 100m Medley Relay - Women)
    • Silver – 2023 (100m Backstroke - Women, 200m Backstroke - Women, 50m Backstroke - Women)
    • Bronze – 2023 (200m Butterfly - Women)

Quick Facts
  • Daughter of Keith and Loretta Smith
  • Has three brothers, Paul, Mickey and James, and four sisters, Shannon, Robyn, Becca and Erin
  • Smith started competitive swimming at the age of 7 and is currently training and coaching at Western Hills Athletic Club in Austin, Texas
  • Her favorite part of being a swimmer is sharing her passion for the sport by coaching age group swimmers
  • Hobbies include rock climbing, adaptive CrossFit, graphic design, web design, travelling and exploring new coffee shops
Paralympic Experience
  • 3-time Paralympian; 3-time Paralympic medalist (2 silver, 1 bronze)
    • Paralympic Games Paris 2024, 6th (100m Butterfly S9 - Women), 8th (100m Backstroke S9 - Women)
    • Paralympic Games Tokyo 2020, silver (100m Butterfly S9 - Women), 5th (100m Backstroke S9 - Women)
    • Paralympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, silver (4 x 100m Freestyle Relay 34 Points - Women), bronze (4 x 100m Medley Relay 34 Points - Women), 4th (100m Butterfly S9 - Women), 5th (100m Backstroke S9 - Women), 9th (50m Freestyle S9 - Women), 10th (200m Individual Medley SM9 - Women)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2023 – 4th (100m Butterfly S9 - Women), 5th (100m Backstroke S9 - Women, 4 x 100m Freestyle Relay 34 Points - Mixed, 4 x 100m Medley Relay 34 Points - Mixed), 6th (50m Freestyle S9 - Women), 7th (100m Freestyle S9 - Women)
  • Years of participation: 100m Backstroke S9 - Women 2013, 2015, 2019, 2022, 2023; 100m Butterfly S9 - Women 2013, 2015, 2019, 2022, 2023; 100m Freestyle S9 - Women 2013, 2015, 2019, 2022, 2023; 4 x 100m Freestyle Relay 34 Points - Mixed 2022, 2023; 4 x 100m Medley Relay 34 Points - Mixed 2022, 2023; 50m Freestyle S9 - Women 2013, 2015, 2019, 2023; 4 x 100m Freestyle Relay 34 Points - Women 2013, 2019; 4 x 100m Medley Relay 34 Points - Women 2013, 2015, 2019
  • Medals: 7 (3 silver, 4 bronze)
    • Silver – 2022 (4 x 100m Freestyle Relay 34 Points - Mixed); 2019 (4 x 100m Freestyle Relay 34 Points - Women, 4 x 100m Medley Relay 34 Points - Women)
    • Bronze – 2019 (100m Backstroke S9 - Women, 100m Butterfly S9 - Women); 2013 (100m Backstroke S9 - Women, 4 x 100m Freestyle Relay 34 Points - Women)

Quick Facts
  • Daughter of Dan and Margee Smith
  • Has two older siblings, Aileen and Daniel, and one younger brother, Neal
  • Great uncle, Billy Conn, is a former world light heavyweight champion in boxing and her grandfather, Jimmy Smith, is a former World Series champion.
Olympic Experience
  • 1-time Olympian; 2-time Olympic medalist (1 gold, 1 bronze)
    • Olympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, gold (4 x 200m Freestyle Relay - Women), bronze (400m Freestyle - Women), 6th (800m Freestyle - Women)
World Championship Experience
  • Most recent: 2023 – silver (4 x 200m Freestyle Relay - Women)
  • Years of participation: 4 x 200m Freestyle Relay - Women 2015, 2017, 2023; 4 x 200m Freestyle Relay (SC) - Women 2016, 2018, 2022; 400m Freestyle (SC) - Women 2016, 2018, 2022; 400m Individual Medley (SC) - Women 2018, 2022; 800m Freestyle (SC) - Women 2016, 2018, 2022; 200m Freestyle (SC) - Women 2016, 2018; 200m Freestyle - Women 2017; 400m Freestyle - Women 2017; 400m Individual Medley - Women 2017; 800m Freestyle - Women 2017
  • Medals: 12 (4 gold, 4 silver, 4 bronze)
    • Gold – 2017 (4 x 200m Freestyle Relay - Women); 2016 (400m Freestyle (SC) - Women, 800m Freestyle (SC) - Women); 2015 (4 x 200m Freestyle Relay - Women)
    • Silver – 2023 (4 x 200m Freestyle Relay - Women); 2018 (4 x 200m Freestyle Relay (SC) - Women); 2017 (400m Freestyle - Women); 2016 (4 x 200m Freestyle Relay (SC) - Women)
    • Bronze – 2022 (4 x 200m Freestyle Relay (SC) - Women, 400m Freestyle (SC) - Women); 2018 (800m Freestyle (SC) - Women); 2017 (800m Freestyle - Women)

bio.education: St. Stephen's and St. Agnes School Smith College ’15, Psychology
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Enjoys comic books, baking, NHL and NWHL hockey
  • Likes to fix things and do her own wheelchair adjustments and repairs
  • Became paralyzed in May of 2014 due to hereditary spastic paraplegia
  • In January of 2016, she was at a workout class at MedStar National Rehabilitation Hospital in Washington, D.C., and the rowing coach approached her and asked if she wanted to try the erg
  • Two weeks later, she won her category at the Mid-Atlantic Erg Sprints, and a month-and-a-half after that, she hit the USRowing elite standard time for a 1k (the Paralympic distance at the time)
  • Daughter of Joe and Vicky Smith and sister of Naval flight officer Tom Smith.
Paralympic Experience
  • 1-time Paralympian
    • Paralympic Games Tokyo 2020, 10th (PR1 Single Sculls - Women)
World Championship Experience
  • Most recent: 2019 – 6th (PR1 Single Sculls - Women)
  • Years of participation: PR1 Single Sculls - Women 2017, 2018, 2019
  • Medals: 1 (1 bronze)
    • Bronze – 2018 (PR1 Single Sculls - Women)
More

US Rowing

Quick Facts
  • Appeared in an episode of Magnum, P.I., and in the movie "Side Out!"
  • Authored the book, "Kings of the Beach"
Career Highlights
  • Smith has competed in more beach events than any other player (416 total, 105 FIVB)
  • Tied for fifth at the 1996 Atlanta Olympic Games with Carl Henkel
  • Won two World Championships with Randy Stoklos
  • Inducted into the UCLA Sports Hall of Fame in 1991, following a career that included two NCAA titles (1976, '79)
  • Current FIVB Beach Control Committee Technical Delegate
  • DID YOU KNOW
  • ---
  • Credited with 139 career victories, Smith retired with a total winnings of $1,625,860
  • Has 10 FIVB victories and was the FIVB Tour Championship four times, all with Randy Stoklos (1989, 90, 91, 92)
  • After retiring in 2011, his 10 FIVB victories still rank him at 17th all-time, added with domestic wins (129), he ranks second all-time in wins
  • Final career tournament was the 2001 Manhattan Beach Open with George Roumain, duo finished ninth
  • Posted a season high three FIVB World Tour wins in 1992 and 1991. 1992, Smith yielded $103,000 in winnings
  • In 1986, Smith won 17 tournaments, out of 24 competed, on the AVP Tour, all with Randy Stoklos
  • Won bronze at the 1994 Goodwill Games
  • Made his international FIVB debut at the 1987 Rio de Janeiro tournament with Stoklos, duo won the event
  • In his career, played in Rio de Janeiro 10 times, the most out of any location internationally that he played - won five of those tournaments
  • At the 1996 Olympics, lost to eventual gold medalists Karch Kiraly and Kent Steffes in the quarterfinals, tying him for fifth
  • Smith is a member of the UCLA Athletics Hall of Fame (1991), the International Volleyball Hall of Fame (2003) and the California Beach Volleyball Hall of Fame (2002)
  • His No. 22 jersey was the second UCLA volleyball jersey to be retired
  • Former athlete representative of the USA Volleyball Board of Directors, President of the AVP and President of the FIVB Beach Volleyball World Council, BOD member for Big Brothers, Big Sisters and the United States Youth Volleyball League
  • Member of the USAV Men's National Team for four years (1979-82)
Olympic Experience
  • 1-time Olympian
    • Olympic Games Atlanta 1996, 5th (Team - Men)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2001 – 17th (Team - Men)
  • Years of participation: Team - Men 1987, 1989, 1990, 1991, 1992, 1993, 1997, 1999, 2001
  • Medals: 6 (5 gold, 1 bronze)
    • Gold – 1992 (Team - Men); 1991 (Team - Men); 1990 (Team - Men); 1989 (Team - Men); 1987 (Team - Men)
    • Bronze – 1993 (Team - Men)

Quick Facts
  • Daughter of Mark and Kay Smith. Has five brothers: Ryan, Eric, Matthew, William and Bobby
  • In 2010, Smith experienced a sudden onset of facial paralysis and numbness, as well as tingling and weakness on her left side
  • She spent the next five months in the hospital as doctors tried to figure out the cause of her symptoms
  • She was diagnosed with a rare neurological muscle disease called dystonia in January 2012
  • She began swimming in 2013 as part of an aquatic rehabilitation session recommended by her physiotherapist
  • In August 2014, she encountered a setback when she began experiencing seizures and was hospitalized for two months
  • In 2017, as a resident athlete at the U.S. Olympic Training Center in Colorado Springs, Colorado, she set the American, Pan Am and world record in the S4 50-meter butterfly at the 2017 U.S. Paralympic Swimming World Championships Trials
  • Was named to the U.S. Women’s Sled Hockey developmental team in 2015.
Paralympic Experience
  • 2-time Paralympian; 5-time Paralympic medalist (2 gold, 3 silver)
    • Paralympic Games Paris 2024, gold (100m Freestyle S3 - Women, 50m Freestyle S4 - Women), silver (4 x 50m Freestyle Relay 20 Points - Mixed, 4 x 50m Medley Relay 20 Points - Mixed), 9th (150m Individual Medley SM4 - Women, 50m Breaststroke SB3 - Women)
    • Paralympic Games Tokyo 2020, silver (100m Freestyle S3 - Women), 5th (150m Individual Medley SM4 - Women, 50m Breaststroke SB3 - Women), 6th (50m Backstroke S3 - Women)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2023 – bronze (200m Freestyle S3 - Women), 5th (100m Freestyle S3 - Women), 6th (50m Freestyle S3 - Women), 7th (150m Individual Medley SM3 - Women), 8th (50m Breaststroke SB3 - Women), 10th (50m Backstroke S3 - Women)
  • Years of participation: 100m Freestyle S3 - Women 2019, 2022, 2023; 150m Individual Medley SM3 - Women 2022, 2023; 200m Freestyle S3 - Women 2022, 2023; 50m Backstroke S3 - Women 2019, 2022, 2023; 50m Breaststroke SB3 - Women 2019, 2022, 2023; 50m Freestyle S3 - Women 2022, 2023; 200m Freestyle S4 - Women 2022; 4 x 50m Medley Relay 20 Points - Mixed 2022; 150m Individual Medley SM4 - Women 2019; 4 x 50m Freestyle Relay 20 Points - Mixed 2017; 50m Butterfly S5 - Women 2017; 50m Freestyle S4 - Women 2017
  • Medals: 12 (10 gold, 1 silver, 1 bronze)
    • Gold – 2022 (100m Freestyle S3 - Women, 150m Individual Medley SM3 - Women, 200m Freestyle S3 - Women, 4 x 50m Medley Relay 20 Points - Mixed, 50m Backstroke S3 - Women, 50m Breaststroke SB3 - Women, 50m Freestyle S3 - Women); 2019 (100m Freestyle S3 - Women, 150m Individual Medley SM4 - Women, 50m Breaststroke SB3 - Women)
    • Silver – 2019 (50m Backstroke S3 - Women)
    • Bronze – 2023 (200m Freestyle S3 - Women)

Quick Facts
  • Competed at the University of Florida
  • Smith studied physiology and kinesiology

  • Said he would still work in elite athletics as a trainer or massage therapist if he was not swimming

  • Was named the SEC's "Male Swimmer of the Year" during the 2019-2020 collegiate season
  • Is a talented musician and often brings a cello with him to meets
Olympic Experience
  • 2-time Olympian; 2-time Olympic medalist (1 silver, 1 bronze)
    • Olympic Games Paris 2024, silver (4 x 200m Freestyle Relay - Men), 11th (400m Freestyle - Men)
    • Olympic Games Tokyo 2020, bronze (400m Freestyle - Men), 4th (4 x 200m Freestyle Relay - Men), 6th (200m Freestyle - Men)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2023 – silver (4 x 200m Freestyle Relay - Men), 7th (200m Freestyle - Men), 9th (400m Freestyle - Men)
  • Years of participation: 200m Freestyle - Men 2023; 4 x 200m Freestyle Relay - Men 2023; 400m Freestyle - Men 2023; 200m Freestyle (SC) - Men 2021, 2022; 4 x 100m Freestyle Relay (SC) - Men 2022; 4 x 100m Medley Relay (SC) - Men 2022; 4 x 200m Freestyle Relay (SC) - Men 2018, 2021, 2022; 4 x 50m Medley Relay (SC) - Men 2022; 400m Freestyle (SC) - Men 2021, 2022; 200m Individual Medley (SC) - Men 2021; 4 x 50m Freestyle Relay (SC) - Men 2021; 400m Individual Medley (SC) - Men 2018, 2021
  • Medals: 7 (4 gold, 2 silver, 1 bronze)
    • Gold – 2022 (4 x 100m Medley Relay (SC) - Men, 4 x 200m Freestyle Relay (SC) - Men, 400m Freestyle (SC) - Men); 2021 (4 x 200m Freestyle Relay (SC) - Men)
    • Silver – 2023 (4 x 200m Freestyle Relay - Men); 2022 (4 x 50m Medley Relay (SC) - Men)
    • Bronze – 2022 (4 x 100m Freestyle Relay (SC) - Men)
